name: TTFB Under Threshold
description: Measures Time To First Byte. AI crawlers make one request and move on — slow TTFB risks crawler abandonment; traditional SEO treats TTFB > 3s as Poor.

criteria.pass: With Lighthouse: TTFB < 800ms measured. Without Lighthouse: WebFetch responds within expected timeframe (no timeout)
criteria.fail: With Lighthouse: TTFB > 2000ms. Without Lighthouse: WebFetch times out or shows significant delay

fix_template: Optimize server response — caching, CDN, database query optimization, reduce server-side processing
sources: AI crawlers make one request and move on. TTFB > 800ms risks AI crawler abandonment. Target < 200ms for optimal AI crawler access. Traditional SEO: TTFB > 3s is "Poor" per Google.

name: No Render-Blocking Resources
description: Verifies the head contains no synchronous scripts or unconditioned stylesheets that block initial render. Render-blocking resources delay LCP — a minor ranking signal through Core Web Vitals.
method_detail: Script without async/defer, stylesheet in head

criteria.pass: No `<script>` tags without `async` or `defer` in `<head>`, no `<link rel="stylesheet">` without `media` queries in `<head>` that block initial render
criteria.fail: 3+ render-blocking scripts or stylesheets in head

fix_template: Add `async` or `defer` to script tags. Use `media="print"` with `onload` swap for non-critical CSS.
sources: Render-blocking resources delay LCP. Minor ranking signal through CWV. More important for user experience than rankings.

name: Images Use Modern Formats
description: Verifies images are served as WebP/AVIF/SVG rather than legacy JPEG/PNG. Modern formats reduce image weight 25-50%, improving LCP and Core Web Vitals.
method_detail: Check img src extensions for webp/avif

criteria.warn: Mix of modern and legacy formats (.jpg, .png)
criteria.fail: All images are legacy formats with no `<picture>` fallback

fix_template: Convert images to WebP. Use `` element for fallback: ``
sources: WebP/AVIF reduce image weight 25-50% vs JPEG/PNG. Reduces page weight, improves LCP. Not a direct ranking signal but contributes to CWV thresholds.

name: Lazy Loading on Below-Fold Images
description: Verifies below-fold images use native lazy loading to reduce initial page weight. Above-fold images must NOT be lazy-loaded (that hurts LCP).
method_detail: Check loading="lazy" on images after first 2

criteria.pass: Images after the first 2 have `loading="lazy"` attribute
criteria.warn: Some below-fold images lack lazy loading
criteria.fail: No lazy loading on any images (and page has 5+ images)

fix_template: Add `loading="lazy"` to all img tags except the first 1-2 (hero/above-fold)
sources: Lazy loading reduces initial page weight. Do NOT lazy-load above-fold images (hurts LCP). Native `loading="lazy"` supported by all modern browsers.

name: No Large Inline CSS/JS Blocks
description: Verifies the page has no large inline style/script blocks. Large inline blocks bloat the HTML document, slowing the initial response; they should be external and cacheable.
fix_template: Move inline CSS/JS to external files with proper caching headers
sources: Large inline blocks increase HTML document size, slow TTFB for the HTML response. Should be external and cacheable.

name: DOM Depth Not Excessive
description: Measures maximum DOM nesting depth. Deep DOM increases rendering cost; ranking impact is negligible but UX impact is measurable on low-end devices.
fix_template: Flatten nested divs. Reduce wrapper elements. Use CSS Grid/Flexbox instead of nested containers.
sources: Deep DOM increases rendering cost. Google Lighthouse flags depth > 32 as excessive. Practical impact on rankings: negligible. UX impact: measurable on low-end devices.

name: Compression Enabled
description: Verifies the response is served with gzip or Brotli compression. Compression cuts transfer size 60-80%; AI crawlers make 3.6x more requests than traditional crawlers, so uncompressed responses risk timeouts at scale.
fix_template: Enable Gzip/Brotli at server/CDN level. Most hosting platforms support this via configuration, not code changes.
sources: Compression reduces transfer size 60-80%. AI crawlers making 3.6x more requests than traditional crawlers — uncompressed responses increase server load and risk timeouts at scale.

name: Cache-Control Headers
description: Verifies the response carries a Cache-Control header with a positive max-age. Caching reduces repeat-visit load times and server load; a UX/infrastructure benefit, not a ranking signal.

criteria.warn: Cache-Control present but max-age = 0 or no-cache

fix_template: Add `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600` (or longer for static assets)
sources: Caching reduces repeat-visit load times and server load. Not a ranking signal. UX and infrastructure benefit.

name: No Mixed Content
description: Verifies no http:// resources are loaded on an HTTPS page. Mixed content triggers browser warnings, Chrome blocks mixed active content, and it erodes HTTPS trust signals.
method_detail: Scan for http:// in src/href on HTTPS page

criteria.pass: Zero instances of `http://` in `src`, `href`, `action` attributes on an HTTPS page
criteria.fail: Any `http://` resource loaded on HTTPS page

fix_template: Replace all `http://` references with `https://` or protocol-relative `//`
sources: Mixed content triggers browser warnings. Chrome blocks mixed active content (scripts). Erodes trust signals. Google considers HTTPS implementation quality.

name: Core Web Vitals Indicators
description: Measures LCP, CLS, and INP against Google's Good/Needs-Improvement/Poor thresholds. CWV is a threshold signal, not a gradient — the ranking benefit caps at Good.
method_detail: Chrome MCP performance API (LCP, CLS, INP)

criteria.pass: With Lighthouse: LCP <= 2.5s AND CLS <= 0.1 AND INP <= 200ms (all "Good")
criteria.warn: With Lighthouse: any metric in "Needs Improvement" range
criteria.fail: With Lighthouse: any metric in "Poor" range (LCP > 4s OR CLS > 0.25 OR INP > 500ms)
criteria.na: Without Lighthouse: cannot measure — note in report

fix_template: Depends on failing metric. LCP: optimize largest image/text block. CLS: add dimensions to images/embeds. INP: reduce JavaScript execution time.
sources: CWV is a threshold signal, not a gradient. Benefit caps at "Good" — no ranking boost beyond. Pages at #1 only 10% more likely to pass CWV than #9 (Dollar Pocket 10M study). INP replaced FID March 2024. Measured at 75th percentile of CrUX field data, not Lighthouse lab data.

name: Image Dimensions Specified
description: Verifies img tags declare explicit width and height. Images without dimensions are the #1 cause of CLS because the browser cannot reserve space before the image loads.
method_detail: Check img tags for width/height attributes

criteria.pass: All `` tags have explicit `width` and `height` attributes (or CSS equivalent)

fix_template: Add `width` and `height` attributes matching the image's intrinsic dimensions: ``
sources: Images without explicit dimensions are the #1 cause of CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift). Browser cannot reserve space until image loads, causing layout shift. Lighthouse flags this as a CLS contributor. All three major tools (Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, Lighthouse) check this.
